The current memory extraction plan is to use prompt‑based extraction with free‑tier hosted LLMs and open‑source embeddings (ensuring reproducibility by using the same embedding model, chunk size, LLM, and question set), with fine‑tuning postponed until after the v0 benchmark and baseline results are complete.
The plan to fine‑tune a 7B model for all memory extraction tasks was rejected for v0.

Fine‑tuning a 7B model was not feasible for v0 because the available local compute resources are CPU‑only, which lack the necessary GPU capacity to handle a model of that size. Consequently, the fine‑tuning plan had to be postponed until after the v0 benchmark and baseline results are completed.
The group set a cost constraint that no paid APIs should be required for the benchmark prototype.

No. The current plan is to use prompt‑based extraction with free‑tier hosted LLMs and open‑source embeddings rather than training all baselines from scratch.

The current monitoring plan replaces the obsolete monthly‑only accuracy check with a weekly drift monitoring routine that computes the Population Stability Index (PSI) for key numerical and categorical features. The v0 dashboard displays the drift status, the time of the last check, and whether model retraining is recommended.
The old monitoring plan checked only model accuracy once per month.

PSI was added to monitor data drift in key numerical and categorical features on a weekly basis, providing a more granular and proactive signal for when the model may need to be retrained. The earlier plan only checked accuracy monthly, which was deemed insufficient, so PSI was introduced to detect drift and trigger retraining if the PSI exceeds the alert threshold for two consecutive checks.
Retraining should be triggered when the PSI (Population Stability Index) crosses the alert threshold for two consecutive checks.
The v0 dashboard should show drift status, the last check time, and whether retraining is recommended.
No. The monthly accuracy‑only monitoring plan is considered obsolete; the team has moved to a more comprehensive plan that includes weekly PSI calculations for key features.
The monitoring plan that computes PSI weekly for key numerical and categorical features superseded the monthly accuracy‑only plan.
Insufficient evidence.

The current policy no longer allows unrestricted automatic shell commands. Only read‑only commands may be executed automatically; any command that writes, modifies, or deletes files or other artifacts requires explicit user approval.
The initial shell command policy permitted the coding agent to execute shell commands automatically after planning, without requiring user approval.

The policy was changed to improve safety and protect generated artifacts. The original policy (E001) let the coding agent run any shell command automatically, but because some commands can modify or delete files (E002) and unrestricted automatic execution was deemed too risky (E005), the policy was tightened (E003) so that only read‑only commands run automatically, while write or delete actions now require explicit user approval.
Network calls are allowed only when the task explicitly requires external data; otherwise they are disabled.
Each tool call must be logged with a timestamp, the command category, and the approval status.
No, unrestricted automatic shell execution is no longer valid.
The unrestricted automatic shell command policy was superseded.
Insufficient evidence.
